events.description: For consistency can we change this to a lowercase `o` in order to ensure compatibility with pre-exis

details.body:
For consistency can we change this to a lowercase `o` in order to ensure compatibility with pre-existing SDKs that customers may be using? i.e. `onCertificateConflictAction`.

OR, is this something that happens automatically when we use TypeSpec conversion. i.e. That it converts all types to capital? If that's the case, is it possible that some other types for exist resources may have been capitalized, and might this potentially cause problems for existing customers SDKs?

Not a worry, read only fields can be expected in PUT requests they are just ignored by RP.

details.body:
It doesn't even need to be semantic, it just needs to do more than compare line by line. The current approach is problematic when one side has a line added somewhere in the middle of the file but no differences otherwise -- with the current algorithm, every line below the added line gets marked as a difference when it really shouldn't be.

Maybe the diff calculation can be outsourced to an existing tool/library (git diff maybe somehow? I don't know)
